item.title: Re: SCP-3322, 'Sister Cities'
item.content:encoded:
How did the foundation discover this.

Surely some major incident probably would of happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a lovely article - fascinating and subtle. It reminds me a little of my own [[SCP-908]] as a geographic anomaly, and I mean that as a sincere compliment. Well done.

item.title: External influences
item.content:encoded: If Mongolia for some reason went to war with China or Russia and the town was destroyed would Argentina go to war with one of there neighbors and the town would also be destroyed by the attacking nation?

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

There is quite a bit going on in this article, and not a whole lot of places to squeeze in additional links without the result being a little contrived, I feel. However, the nods to MTF Epsilon-6 and the GOC can easily link out to their hub pages. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

After deliberation by Site ██ staff, an operation to apprehend this official is conducted on 23/11/2015 by MTF Epsilon-6. She identifies herself as a GOC field operative, 'Captain Polina Pavlovna'. While she refuses to divulge any other useful information, intelligence from other sources confirms the ANG unit seen at SCP-3322-A was a GOC strike team, whose operation was planned at least 1 month in advance.

To this:

After deliberation by Site ██ staff, an operation to apprehend this official is conducted on 23/11/2015 by MTF Epsilon-6. She identifies herself as a GOC field operative, 'Captain Polina Pavlovna'. While she refuses to divulge any other useful information, intelligence from other sources confirms the ANG unit seen at SCP-3322-A was a GOC strike team, whose operation was planned at least 1 month in advance.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was also reminded of that article, and not in a bad way! That's a really good skip to be compared to, and this was well executed, not to mention just charming as heck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this causality stuff, and also like how this operative is blaming the affair on the scip.

Hearty upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Google maps, probably.

Good to know, since I was trying to invoke that effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm curious as to how anyone even noticed the SCP here. How did this even come under the foundation's attention. Also makes me wonder, out of billions of other people on earth how many are , by chance are living the exact life I am.

item.title: Re: SCP-3322, 'Sister Cities'
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for reading. My rationale for using Buenos Aires was that there would be a Foundation office there (perhaps not in the others) in order to debrief/ supervise/bring get well soon cards.

As to the town choice, I used this to pick the sites (looking for two towns of similar size and isolation), but converting the farm was the best I could find for the kind of environment I had pictured.

item.title: Re: SCP-3322, 'Sister Cities'
item.content:encoded:
Argentinian here! I was going to ask you if Agent Santos was a reference to the Argentine show "Los Simuladores", which would've been awesome, but I guess it was just a serendipity. Very well done!
What I'm not sure if you'd really need to fly to Buenos Aires from Santa Cruz just for a head injury, given that there are mayor cities closer (Río Gallegos and Comodoro Rivadavia would be better options), but I also understand the need of dropping a big city name to make people understand the geographical context.
I would've like to see a real city being used as an SCP (the real Cameron is a ranch) just to make it more realistic, but I'm just being nitpicky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this.

There's something about how it's all so strange and yet so mundane that really works for me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agent Navalny escalates the decision for review, reporting that Agent Santos initiated the relationship 2 days before he did and his own actions were thus conducted under the influence of an SCP.

Amazing.

I definitely think the article could use some trimming in the middle, but man the event logs were great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks - I think dropping SCP-3322 from everything is definitely an improvement and I've adjusted the descriptions.

I prefer to keep both names and designations as it ties into the theme of being both ordinary people and SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The premise is relatively simple but the article built on it is detailed and well thought out. Solid upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the number and length of designations may be contributing to this. Perhaps there's a cleaner way to name everything?

+ show block
– hide block

Would it be feasible to simply cut "SCP-3322-" when discussing residents, and rearrange what's left to place their location over their individual designations (For ease of reference, I figure it'd flow easier to know immediately which town they belonged to rather than which resident they are of the town.)?

For the agents, maybe a symbol could be used to further differentiate them from residents? Also, unless the names are super important, I'd consider cutting them; see how that affects readability.

So:

SCP-3322-1568-A (Mr O. Vidal), wielding a hunting rifle, shoots and kills his wife, SCP-3322-1569-A (Mrs M. Vidal), during a heated argument. SCP-3322-0-B (Agent B. Ganbold) is informed of these events 2 hours later by SCP-3322-0-A (Agent G. Martin) and he investigates the residence of SCP-3322-1568-B (Mr A. Gantulga) and SCP-3322-1569-B (Ms O. Bayarmaa).

Would look like:

A-1568, wielding a hunting rifle, shoots and kills his wife, A-1569, during a heated argument. Ω-B is informed of these events 2 hours later by Ω-A and he investigates the residence of B-1568 and B-1569.

This:

SCP-3322-0-A (Agent E. Santos) and B (Agent V. Navalny) are discovered to have been involved in an intimate relationship with SCP-3322-491-A (Ms C. Herrera) and B (Ms O. Erdenchimeg) for the previous 3 months.

Would look like:

Ω-A and Ω-B are discovered to have been involved in an intimate relationship with A-491 and B-491 for the previous 3 months.

(Hell, you could also call the personnel: Operative-B and Operative-A)

Operative-A and Operative-B are discovered to have been involved in an intimate relationship with A-491 and B-491 for the previous 3 months.

This:

SCP-3322-A. SCP-3322-2201-A and SCP-3322-3977-A visible in foreground.

Would appear as:

SCP-3322-A. A-2201 and A-3977 visible in foreground.

In cases where name matters, simple rewording should provide sufficient context:

On 1/03/1995, SCP-3322-1-B (Mayor C. Batu) announces he will use his personal funds to construct a house for the use of SCP-3322-0-B (Agent B. Ganbold)

On 1/03/1995, the mayor of SCP-3322-B announces he will use his personal funds to construct a house for the use of Ω-B.

Hell, maybe you can drop the designations altogether in some places and go with names instead:

Mr O. Vidal, wielding a hunting rifle, shoots and kills his wife during a heated argument. Agent B. Ganbold is informed of these events 2 hours later by Agent G. Martin and he investigates the residence of the corresponding couple in Buyant.

Agents E. Santos and V. Navalny are each discovered to have been involved in an intimate relationship with corresponding civilians for the previous 3 months.

On 1/03/1995, the mayor of Buyant announces he will use his personal funds to construct a house for the use of Operative B.

Not perfect, but you could stand to play around with it until you find something workable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotcha, and no worries, using a pre-exisiting MTF isn't bad form at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for updating the MTF list. I'd prefer that you didn't add a link in my article - I see most articles don't link back to that page even when they use the MTF heavily throughout, so I don't really want people to think that this SCP has a hidden important connection to the MTF when they see a conspicuous link there.

On the other hand I don't want to claim credit for other people's MTFs, so let me know if this is bad form.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I keep the list updated on Wrongjohn's behalf, and have added your usage accordingly! Would you mind if I made the mention in your article a crosslink to the MTf's position on the hub?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed that a lot, and it did cross my mind because of the South American connection (although to be honest the location was just chosen because the list of places at antipodes is pretty small)

That is indeed the MTF. Is there some etiquette as to whether I should be updating that list? I wasn't sure if those were intended to link to SCPs where you can actually learn more about the MTF, or every single mention of that MTF should be added.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree that the walls of text are not great in this, but I don't know if there is a better way to present the information or anything in particular to cut out. I've made some edits for formatting and collapsed the last section, which will hopefully help a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but it still feels messy with how huge the paragraphs feel. Neutral vote for now, but leaning towards positive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's well written and clearly portraited and the idea is original, but most pharagraphs are just huge and it really does't encorages on reading it. But very good, nonetheless!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice. Reminded me of Kalinin's skip about the man who had a similar relationship with a town, but the directions you went with yours were quite different. Is the epsilon 6 mentioned here intended to be the Village idiots?

item.title: SCP-3322, 'Sister Cities'
item.content:encoded:
I feel like something like this has already been done, but if so maybe that's apt.

Image sources: 1, 2, 3

Happy to take suggestions from anyone who has a better understanding of Argentine/Mongolian culture than my 'I went to Buenos Aires once'.
